hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to better complement SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 contacts, and hydrophobic residues at position 11 to avoid off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the native SST-14 pharmacophore. This iteration supports the hypothesis but shows limited progress in increasing Δmargin beyond the current best.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the critical FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14 pharmacophore.

These mutations introduce charged or polar residues at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. They are unlikely to interfere with the native SST-14 pharmacophore.
